ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Lemon Rice. Nearest parent is a tempered rice dish. A new concept covering South Indian variety rice dishes is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tempered/seasoned rice dish, South Indian style (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of lemon rice is 1 plate (approximately 250g) — a bright yellow, fluffy rice tossed with a tangy lemon tempering, garnished with roasted peanuts and curry leaves, served at room temperature.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Rice — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code F05100073; cross-referenced with ICMR-NIN
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore; Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match in HPB Foodo under food code F05100073 — Lemon Rice, South Indian style tempered preparation.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from cooked white rice; lemon juice and fat tempering moderately lower GI vs plain cooked rice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 495mg per plate (250g) — moderate; primarily from added salt. Lemon juice provides potassium and is naturally sodium-free.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable for hypertensive patients when prepared with reduced salt. Lemon rice is naturally low in saturated fat and cholesterol-free.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~66); glycaemic load per full plate (250g) is approximately 54 — high. The peanuts and tempering fat provide modest moderation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit to 150g (about half a standard plate) and pair with a protein-rich side such as yogurt or dal. Vitamin C from lemon juice may improve iron absorption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Turmeric in the tempering provides curcumin with potent anti-inflammatory properties; lemon juice provides vitamin C which supports collagen synthesis and iron absorption.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis. The turmeric and peanut content provide anti-inflammatory benefits. Vitamin C from lemon juice enhances iron absorption from other foods in the meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Rice is a classic South Indian variety rice dish — cooked white rice tossed with a tangy, citrus-bright tempering of oil, mustard seeds, urad dal, chana dal, curry leaves, dried red chilli, turmeric, and fresh lemon juice, garnished with roasted peanuts. The turmeric turns the rice a vibrant yellow, and the lemon provides a sharp, refreshing acidity. Often served at room temperature, lemon rice is a popular packed lunch, temple prasad offering, and festival food across Tamil Nadu and Karnataka. In Singapore, it is served at South Indian restaurants as a specialty rice dish.
common_user_queries: lemon rice vs plain rice nutrition

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code F05100073 — Lemon Rice, South Indian tempered preparation.
notes: Contains peanut allergen. Vegan and gluten-free. Cholesterol is 0.0. Vitamin C from fresh lemon juice degrades if the dish is held warm for extended periods. GI and glycaemic load per serving are high due to the white rice base.
